title: HRW/PHR Report: 52 Deaths in ICE Custody in 500 Days; Mortality Rate Highest in 20 Years
excerpt: A joint 73-page report by Human Rights Watch and Physicians for Human Rights documented 52 deaths in ICE custody in the first 500 days of the Trump administration, finding the mortality rate had more than doubled since Trump took office, exceeded Covid-19 pandemic levels, and was nearly four times the rate under the Biden administration.
content:
On June 25, 2026, Human Rights Watch and Physicians for Human Rights jointly published Dying in Detention: Rising Deaths in an Expanding US Immigration Detention System, a 73-page report analyzing 52 deaths in ICE custody from January 20, 2025 through June 4, 2026.

  • The report found the ICE detention mortality rate had more than doubled since Trump's second term began, reaching its highest level in nearly 20 years — nearly four times the Biden administration rate and higher than the peak rate during the Covid-19 pandemic.
  • The increase in deaths was disproportionate to population growth: the detained population increased 77% from approximately 40,000 to over 71,000, but the annualized mortality rate rose 138%, demonstrating deaths could not be explained by detention growth alone.
  • PHR medical experts found a high suspicion of inadequate or delayed health care in multiple cases reviewed, raising concerns that deaths may have been preventable.
  • The report documented seven apparent suicides from January 2025 to January 2026, compared to one in all of 2024, suggesting systemic failure to monitor and respond to mental health crises.
  • HRW and PHR warned that oversight mechanisms within DHS had been simultaneously dismantled as the detained population surged, creating a compounding crisis.

The report concluded that the surge in deaths was 'a foreseeable outcome' of expanded mandatory detention, overcrowding, deficient medical staffing, and the dismantling of oversight — and called on Congress to establish mandatory independent investigations of every in-custody death.
source: Human Rights Watch / Physicians for Human Rights
mediaPlaceholder: 73-page HRW/PHR report cover and mortality rate chart showing spike above Covid-era levels
